workflow.name: Deposit Allocation Correction
workflow.description:
# UC5: Deposit Allocation Correction

Corrects an incorrect deposit allocation by reversing the original posting and re-allocating to the correct consumer. Triggered when an analyst identifies a misrouted deposit during reconciliation or investigation.

## Steps

1. **Reverse Incorrect Allocation** (T5501) -- DR Hold (20101), CR per-file Clearing (10101); reverse the allocation ledger mirror. Precondition: consumer must still have funds on Hold.
2. **Re-allocate Correctly** (T5101) -- Post the correct consumer's share using the standard allocation template. DR Clearing, CR Hold for the right consumer.

**Final state:** The incorrect consumer's Hold and allocation positions are reversed. The correct consumer now holds the deposit.

steps.description: Reverse the incorrect allocation: debit Customer Hold (20101) and credit per-file Clearing (10101) on the economic ledger; debit Consumer FBO Clearing (21101) and credit Cash Balances – Clearing (11901) on the allocation ledger. Precondition: debits on 20101 must not exceed credits (consumer must still have funds on hold).

Re-allocate to the correct consumer: debit per-file Clearing (10101) and credit Customer Hold (20101) on the economic ledger; debit Cash Balances – Clearing (11901) and credit Customer Master FBO Clearing (21101) on the allocation ledger.
steps.condition_description: Fires when the reversal was for a misallocation (wrong consumer, wrong amount) rather than a duplicate. The correct destination must be determined before this step fires.
